zh.Court of Hill, Whitemere Castle, Ellesme: 英國史洛普爾艾勒斯莫
zh.Booton, Norfolk, England: 英國英格蘭諾福爾可布托
zh.Poynton, Macclesfield, Cheshire, England: 英國爾馬可萊斯費爾德
zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Margaret Warren於1378年出生在英國爾馬可萊斯費爾德時，她的父親Sir John II de Warenne, Baron of Poynton and Stockport35歲，母親Margaret Stafford33歲。她於1397年與Sir Griffith Hill在英國蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1452年在英國史洛普爾艾勒斯莫享年74歲，埋葬在英國史洛普爾廷頓。

zh-hans.Poynton, Macclesfield, Cheshire, England: 英国爾馬可萊斯費爾德
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Margaret Warren于1378年出生在英国爾馬可萊斯費爾德时，她的父亲Sir John II de Warenne, Baron of Poynton and Stockport35岁，母亲Margaret Stafford33岁。她于1397年与Sir Griffith Hill在英国苏格兰结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1452年在英国史洛普爾艾勒斯莫享年74岁，埋葬在英国史洛普爾廷頓。

nameMeanings.name: Warren
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English (Old French) personal name Warin, Werin, a borrowing of ancient Germanic Warino, a short form of various compound names based on the element warin ‘protection, shelter’ or ‘guard’. Compare Waring .

English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from La Varrenne in Seine-Maritime, France, named with a Gaulish element probably descriptive of alluvial land or sandy soil. This was the name of a major Norman family after the Conquest. In Ireland, this name has been Gaelicized as Bharain.
Irish: adopted as an English form of Gaelic Ó Murnáin (see Murnane , Warner ).

variants.name: Warrens
meanings: English: variant of Warren with genitival or excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Warrener
meanings: English (of Norman origin): occupational name from Old French warrennier ‘warrener’, an officer employed to watch over game in a park or game preserve. Compare Warren .

variants.name: Murnane
meanings: Irish (Munster): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Murnáin, a shortened and dissimilated form of Ó Manannáin ‘descendant of Manannán’, name borne in Celtic mythology by a sea god associated with the Isle of Man. All three syllables appear in the variant Marrinan. This name has also been Anglicized as Warren and Warner .

variants.name: Warne
meanings: occasionally a habitational name from Wearne in Somerset. The place takes its name from a lost stream-name based on British verno- ‘alder, swamp’.

variants.name: Waring
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): variant of Warren , from the Norman personal name Warin, derived from ancient Germanic Warino, a short form of various compound names based on the element warin ‘protection, shelter’ or ‘guard’. The name was popular in France and among the Normans (see Guerin ).

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Margaret Warren was born in 1378, in Macclesfield, Cheshire, England, her father, Sir John II de Warenne, Baron of Poynton and Stockport, was 35 and her mother, Margaret Stafford, was 33. She married Sir Griffith Hill in 1397, in Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died in 1452, in Ellesmere, Shropshire, England, at the age of 74, and was buried in Whittington, Shropshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Margaret Warren est née en 1378, à Macclesfield, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir John II de Warenne, Baron of Poynton and Stockport, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Margaret Stafford, avait 33 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Griffith Hill en 1397, à Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1452, à Ellesmere, Shropshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 74 ans, et a été enterrée à Whittington, Shropshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Margaret Warren 1378 in Macclesfield, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir John II de Warenne, Baron of Poynton and Stockport 35 und ihre Mutter Margaret Stafford 33. Sie hat 1397 in Schottland, United Kingdom Sir Griffith Hill geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1452 im Alter von 74 Jahren in Ellesmere, Shropshire, England und wurde in Whittington, Shropshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Margaret Warren è nata il nel 1378 nel Macclesfield, Cheshire, England, sua padre, Sir John II de Warenne, Baron of Poynton and Stockport, aveva 35 anni e sua madre, Margaret Stafford, aveva 33 anni. Lei sposato Sir Griffith Hill nel 1397, nel Scozia, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1452, nel Ellesmere, Shropshire, England, all'età di 74 anni, e fu sepolta a Whittington, Shropshire, England.

ja.Poynton, Macclesfield, Cheshire, England: イギリスチェシュアイアマククレスフィールド
ja.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Warrenが1378年にイギリスチェシュアイアマククレスフィールドで生まれた時，父Sir John II de Warenne, Baron of Poynton and Stockportは35歳，母Margaret Staffordは33歳であった。1397年にイギリススコットランドでSir Griffith Hillと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1452年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアエレスメアで74歳で死去し，イギリスシュロプシュアイアウィッティントンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Warren(이)가 1378년 영국 체실르 맥클스필드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John II de Warenne, Baron of Poynton and Stockport(은)는 35세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Stafford(은)는 33세였다. 그녀는 1397년 영국 스코틀랜드에서 Sir Griffith Hill(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1452년 영국 슈럽실르 엘레스머에서 74세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 슈럽실르 휘틴그턴에 묻혔다.

mn.Booton, Norfolk, England: Booton, Norðfolc, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Margaret Warren nasceu em 1378, em Macclesfield, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John II de Warenne, Baron of Poynton and Stockport, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Stafford, tinha 33 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Griffith Hill em 1397, em Escócia, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1452, em Ellesmere, Shropshire, Inglaterra, com 74 anos, e foi sepultada em Whittington, Shropshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Margaret Warren родилась в 1378 г. в местности макклесфиелд, чешир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John II de Warenne, Baron of Poynton and Stockport было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Stafford, 33. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Griffith Hill в 1397 г. в местности Шотландия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1452 г. в местности эллесмир, шропшир, Англия в возрасте 74 лет и была похоронена в местности уиттингтон, шропшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Margaret Warren nació en 1378, en Macclesfield, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John II de Warenne, Baron of Poynton and Stockport, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Margaret Stafford, tenía 33 años. Se casó con Sir Griffith Hill en 1397, en Escocia, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió en 1452, en Ellesmere, Shropshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 74 años, y fue sepultada en Whittington, Shropshire, Inglaterra.
